other_observers: Chris Wood, Coen Dexter, Brenda Wright; I don't know who first found the birds
specific_location: cemetery on the east end of the town of Norwood
description: All eight birds looked very similar, so I here describe them as one. A large, plump dove, it was basically soft brown above and pale pinkish-brown below. It had a distinct black half-collar, bordered above and below by thin white bands, on the back of the neck. The primaries were the darkest part of the perched bird, except for the blackish base to the tail. That black tail base extended only to the tips of the undertail coverts, except for the outer web of the R6s, where the black extended a bit beyond the undertail coverts.  In flight, the wings were tri-colored -- soft brown interior (lesser coverts), pale gray middle (median and greater coverts), and blackish outer (primaries). The bill was black, the eyes were red, and the feet were pinkish-red.Behaviors: Perched on power line and flyingCall: None heardPlumage: Basic
